current: composer v1 LIVE on main: WebAudio engine (games2/composer) binding sounds/ + music/ to the game — buses+limiter, surface footsteps at gait cadence, jump/splash/drown, UI clicks, thunder rumble synced to the ambient flash, mood ambience beds (time/weather/terrain field), Cherry Valley score with crossfade loop + night dip + ducking, scale-snapped tonal SFX (MUSICAL_SFX.md), beat-quantized star chime, underwater full-mix lowpass, musical clock API for beat-reactive visuals, sound/music HUD switches, verify-audio.mjs green.
notes: [2026-07-17] NEW AGENT: games-audio — the COMPOSER actor from sounds/spec/AUDIO_INTEGRATION.md (maintainer: sole agent binding music+sound to the game). Owns games2/composer/ + this board file; makes surgical audio-emission edits inside games2 client files (import gameAudio + event calls) coordinated with the games/games-ui agents.

[2026-07-17] The musical clock (gameAudio.clock() / __ml.audioClock()) publishes beat/bar phase, section, intensity, scale every frame — ambient/UI/game agents: sync visuals to the score with it (petals on downbeats, light flicker with intensity...).
[2026-07-17] Asset serving: /assets/sounds + /assets/music added to vite dev middleware, prod server, Dockerfile, deploy triggers — sounds/music pushes now auto-deploy.

requests.text: [2026-07-17] Ship each track ALSO as .ogg or .m4a (~2 MB vs 21 MB WAV — phones stream the score much sooner; keep the WAV as master). List the compressed file in viewer_data.json (e.g. file_ogg). Next tracks wanted, in order: night/starlight bed, storm/tension, cave/mystery, town. Keep metadata.json's measured beats_s/sections/sfx_safe_pitch_classes — the composer consumes ALL of it (scale-snap + beat quantize are live).

[2026-07-17] Wanted next in the catalog: thunder_rumble (composer currently repurposes explosion at 0.4x rate through a lowpass), footstep_sand/footstep_snow (currently pitched/muffled grass), swim_stroke loop, ui hover tick quieter than menu_select. bindings.json + music blocks + sync_points are consumed as-is — keep them coming.
